conditions: Coronary artery disease (history of prior cardiac event)
medications: Glyceryl trinitrate (GTN) spray as needed for angina symptoms
Paracetamol as needed for back pain
health.mental_health: Generally resilient but has periodic anxiety around overexertion and another cardiac event; ongoing stress from repeatedly requesting accessibility accommodations at venues and managing pain flares.
recent_visits: NHS cardiology follow-up with ECG and medication review (last month)
Physiotherapy session for back pain and mobility strengthening (fortnightly)
Audiology appointment for hearing assessment and hearing aid adjustment (two months ago)
GP review for blood pressure and cholesterol labs (six weeks ago)

finance.income_range: £35,000–£60,000 per year (variable freelance income; seasonal peaks)
finance.debt: Small remaining balance on a business equipment loan for camera bodies and lenses; uses a 0% balance transfer card intermittently during low-season months.
recent_transactions: £1,280 deposit received for a July wedding package
£460 payment to physio clinic not fully covered by insurance
£320 annual public liability insurance renewal for events work
£189 train travel (London to Manchester) for a corporate event shoot
£1,750 purchase of a lighter mirrorless camera body to reduce strain

relationships: Partner: Aisha Rahman (lives nearby in East London; helps with post-event recovery days)
Close friend and second-shooter: Tomás Delgado (covers parts of events when Skyler needs breaks)
Sibling: Priya Nair (lives in Croydon; frequent check-ins about health and finances)
Peer network: Disability Arts Online and London-based access advocates (support with venue accessibility)
Long-term clients and referrals from two wedding planners in Hackney and Islington
social.social_media_activity: Maintains an active Instagram portfolio and a small TikTok presence focused on behind-the-scenes wedding work; publicly shares access-positive tips (e.g., asking venues about lifts/quiet rooms) without disclosing personal medical details. Keeps Facebook mostly for client referrals and community groups.
private_conversations: Negotiating accessibility accommodations with venues (step-free access, nearby parking, rest breaks, quiet space)
Discussing fear of another cardiac episode and how it affects taking long bookings
Boundary-setting with clients about day length and the need for a second shooter
Sharing frustrations about occasional misgendering by vendors/clients and strategies for handling it

schedule.work_hours: Typically 20–35 hours/week off-season; 45–60 hours/week during peak wedding season (long weekend shoots plus weekday editing), paced with enforced rest breaks due to disability and cardiac history.
regular_appointments: Physiotherapy every other Tuesday morning (Shoreditch clinic)
Cardiac rehab-style exercise class once weekly (community leisure centre, low-intensity)
Monthly GP check-in or blood pressure monitoring (as needed)
upcoming_events: Wedding at a venue near Richmond (full-day coverage; booked with an assistant) - next Saturday
Corporate awards night at The Brewery, City of London (evening coverage) - in two weeks
Cardiology review and blood tests at St Bartholomew's Hospital - in three weeks
Accessibility walk-through call with a new venue coordinator in Camden - next Wednesday
schedule.daily_routine: Mornings start with medication, blood pressure check, and short mobility stretches; editing and client emails late morning; afternoons for shoots, scouting, or admin; early evening meal and a short walk when energy allows; strict wind-down routine and earlier nights after event days to manage pain and cardiac fatigue.

professional.company: Self-employed sole trader operating under 'Skyler Nair Photography' (London-based), often subcontracting a second shooter and an editor during peak season
projects: Spring wedding season: 6 confirmed weddings with access plans documented per venue
Corporate events retainer with a Shoreditch PR agency (quarterly shoots)
Portfolio refresh focusing on inclusive weddings (LGBTQ+ and multicultural ceremonies)
Workflow overhaul to reduce physical strain (lighter kit, rolling case, more off-site editing)
professional.performance_issues: Occasional need to pause during long ceremonies due to back pain or shortness of breath; mitigated by using a second shooter and building scheduled breaks into contracts. Has had to decline a few high-intensity multi-day events, which affects earnings and client perception if not explained carefully.
workplace_relationships: Strong rapport with two wedding planners who proactively ask venues about access needs
Mixed experiences with some venue coordinators who resist accommodation requests (e.g., parking permits, lift access to balconies)
Trusted collaboration with Tomás Delgado for coverage handoffs and safety check-ins during long shoots

hobbies: Street photography along the South Bank and Spitalfields
Visiting galleries (Tate Modern, Photographers' Gallery) during quieter weekdays
Gentle swimming sessions when pain allows
Cooking South Indian-inspired meals adapted for heart health
habits: Tracks blood pressure and heart rate at home
Uses pacing techniques and scheduled rest breaks to manage disability and fatigue
Carries GTN spray and a medical ID card during shoots
Limits alcohol (rarely drinks at weddings despite being offered)
Avoids heavy lifting; uses a rolling case and requests step-free routes
lifestyle.living_situation: Rents a step-free one-bedroom flat in East London with accessible bathroom adaptations (installed with landlord permission). Keeps a compact home editing setup with an ergonomic chair and a standing-desk converter to reduce back strain; relies on nearby partner and friends for occasional help carrying gear.
lifestyle.dietary_preferences: Heart-healthy, lower-salt diet with more fish, lentils, vegetables, and whole grains; mindful of caffeine on shoot days; tries to keep regular meal times to avoid reflux.
